pr_title: Introduce DAO layer for authors/books and refactor routes to use it
pr_description:
This PR introduces a small DAO layer for authors and books and refactors the Flask routes to use it instead of issuing raw SQL directly from the handlers.

Summary of changes
- Added `dao.py` with `AuthorDAO` and `BookDAO` classes that encapsulate all DB access for authors and books (basic CRUD and list methods).
- Updated author routes in `app.py` (`GET /authors`, `GET /authors/<id>`, `POST /authors`) to use `AuthorDAO`.
- Updated book routes in `app.py` (`GET /books`, `GET /books/<id>`, `POST /books`, `PATCH /books/<id>`, `DELETE /books/<id>`) to use `BookDAO` for all DB operations.
- Added a simple `DELETE /authors/<id>` endpoint using `AuthorDAO.delete` for author removal.

The intent is to keep the existing API surface and behavior the same while making the DB access a bit more structured and easier to evolve in future (e.g. if we ever want to swap out SQLite or add more complex queries).
